article_title: Micron and SK Hynix Join Samsung in the Trillion-Dollar Club as AI Memory Supercycle Reshapes Semiconductor Valuations
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

findings.message: Source redirected to different domain: consent.yahoo.com

content_preview.title: Micron and SK Hynix Join Samsung in the Trillion-Dollar Club as AI Memory Supercycle Reshapes Semiconductor Valuations
content_preview.summary: All three major memory chipmakers crossed $1 trillion in market cap within weeks as AI demand, sold-out HBM4 capacity, and a landmark UBS upgrade triggered a historic valuation surge.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

In the span of a few weeks, all three of the world's major memory chipmakers crossed the $1 trillion market capitalization threshold — a milestone that would have seemed implausible eighteen months ago, when Micron Technology traded at under $100 per share. On May 26, 2026, Micron's stock surged 19.29% to $895.88, carrying its market cap past $1 trillion for the first time in the company's history, [according to Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/markets/article/micron-tops-1-...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-written, substantively strong piece covering a genuinely significant market milestone. Good use of a four-section structure (Overview, What We Know, What We Don't Know, Analysis). Technical depth is appropriate for the News category. The Analysis section correctly identifies the structural shift in memory market dynamics without editorializing.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 source snapshots present. Sources read: source-0.html.gz (Yahoo Finance — redirected to consent.yahoo.com consent page, no article content accessible); source-1.html.gz (American Bazaar Online — confirms Arcuri upgrade, Tongluo/Powerchip Taiwan facility, and the 'We believe the market will start to put a more normal multiple' quote); source-2.html.gz (The Tech Marketer — confirms $1,625 target, 204% revision, $23.9B Q2 revenue 196% YoY, $18.7B guidance beat, EPS $12.07 756% YoY, Q3 $33.5B guidance 260% YoY, $7.7B cloud revenue 163% YoY, HBM4 sold-out, 39 of 44 analysts Buy, 1-alpha DRAM Manassas, DRAM 58-63% / NAND 70-75% price rises, 'no reason why MU should trade a whole lot differently than NVDA in terms of P/E'); source-3.html.gz (Blockonomi — confirms gross margin 74.4% doubling from below 20% in 2023, Q3 gross margin >81%, EPS $19.15 vs consensus $12.05, 14x move in twelve months, 50-65% demand fulfillment, HBM TAM $35B to $100B); source-4.html.gz (Seoul Economic Daily — confirms SK hynix +11.30% to 2.286M won, 1,500 trillion won ~$1T, Samsung combined cap 2,000 trillion won (common 1,847T + preferred 156.6T), Micron +19.3% to $895.88, Kim Young-gun targets raised, EV/EBITDA 6x to 7x quote verbatim, SOCAMM2, ROE 66% 2026-2028); source-5.html.gz (NAND Research — confirms Goldman Sachs 15-year severity, DRAM deficit 4.9%, NAND 4.2%, HBM 5.1%, SK Hynix HBM secured October 2025, 40-week lead times, $755B hyperscaler capex 83% YoY, memory 30% of capex up from 8%, Samsung+SK Hynix 70% DRAM, Samsung+Micron+SK Hynix 62.9% NAND flash market); source-6.html.gz (Motley Fool — confirms $250 downside scenario if HBM demand weakens, Micron at $1T 'among the ten largest U.S. companies'). Yahoo Finance snapshot inaccessible (consent page); two claims attributed solely to Yahoo Finance cannot be verified from snapshot: (1) 'best performance since 2011' for the single-day gain, and (2) the specific claim that a $1.8 trillion market cap would rank Micron 'seventh among all US public companies.' The Motley Fool (source-6) says Micron at $1T is 'among the ten largest U.S. companies,' which is potentially inconsistent with the Yahoo Finance-attributed 'seventh' ranking at $1.8T (though timing differences could explain this).
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core financial claims well-supported: Q2 revenue $23.9B, EPS $12.07, gross margin 74.4%, Q3 guidance $33.5B with >81% gross margin and EPS $19.15 vs $12.05 consensus — all confirmed by Tech Marketer and Blockonomi. SK hynix and Samsung market cap data confirmed by Seoul Economic Daily verbatim. Goldman Sachs deficit figures, supply chain data, and hyperscaler capex figures confirmed by NAND Research. The 14-fold/14x move claim confirmed by Blockonomi. One attribution issue: the article states 'Arcuri wrote, [as quoted by American Bazaar Online]' but American Bazaar says 'the firm wrote' — minor misattribution of individual vs. institutional authorship of a UBS note. The quote text itself is accurate and confirmed. The 'seventh' US ranking claim attributed to Yahoo Finance is not confirmed by any snapshot; Motley Fool describes Micron as 'among the ten largest U.S. companies' at the current $1T level, which is plausible but not equivalent to seventh at $1.8T. The 'best performance since 2011' claim is similarly attributed only to Yahoo Finance with no snapshot confirmation.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism or loaded language. The Analysis section is measured and appropriately flags uncertainty. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: Distinct from existing coverage: the March 2026 article on Micron's Q2 revenue ($239B) covers financial results; this covers the $1 trillion market cap milestone as a market event involving all three memory chipmakers simultaneously. The March HBM4 mass production article is cross-linked appropriately. No duplicate detected.
concerns: Yahoo Finance source-0 snapshot redirected to consent.yahoo.com — no article content is accessible. Two specifics attributed only to Yahoo Finance are unverifiable from the snapshot: (1) 'best performance since 2011' for Micron's single-day gain, and (2) 'seventh among all US public companies by market value' at $1.8T target. These are subordinate body claims, not in the headline or lead. The 'seventh' figure is potentially inconsistent with Motley Fool's 'ten largest U.S. companies' phrasing at the current $1T level.

Five of seven sources (americanbazaaronline.com, thetechmarketer.com, blockonomi.com, en.sedaily.com, nand-research.com) are not on the source allowlist. These are credible secondary outlets whose content was read and verified; the Seoul Economic Daily and NAND Research in particular are authoritative primary sources for Korean market data and NAND supply analysis respectively. The allowlist should be updated.
recommendations: File a corrections record documenting that the Yahoo Finance source was inaccessible (consent redirect) at the time of archiving, and that the 'best performance since 2011' and 'seventh among US companies' claims cannot be independently verified from the snapshot. Both are supportable subordinate claims that do not affect the headline or lead.
Add en.sedaily.com, nand-research.com, thetechmarketer.com, americanbazaaronline.com, and blockonomi.com to config/source_allowlist.txt.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, well-researched submission documenting a genuine market milestone with strong source support across five verifiable snapshots. The Yahoo Finance consent-page redirect is a provenance limitation rather than a fabrication — the core claims are corroborated by other sources. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS: file a corrections record noting the Yahoo Finance snapshot limitation and the unverifiable 'seventh' ranking claim.
